site_title: 주간 AI ｜ AI 활용 기계공학 논문 브리핑

mech.title: Deep Learning Surrogate Models for Nonlinear Magneto-Thermal Analysis of TEAM Problem 36
mech.summary: Induction heating is widely used in industrial processes such as forging, hardening, and additive manufacturing, but its accurate numerical simulation requires coupled electromagnetic and thermal finite element analyses with nonlinear, temperature-dependent material properties. This work proposes a deep learning surrogate model based on a convolu-tional neural network for TEAM Workshop Problem 36, a reference benchmark for nonlinear magneto-thermal induction heating. A database of more than 40,000 finite element solutions was generated by varying the supply current from 2 to 6 kA and the frequency from 2 to 6 kHz, while accounting for transient nonlinear effects, including the Curie transition. The network, composed of 24 layers with transposed convolutions, batch normalization, and dropout, maps current, frequency, and time to radial temperature distributions in the steel billet. For most operating conditions, the model achieves mean absolute percentage errors of about 6–7% for radial line in the middle of the billet and about 10% for radial line close to the billet end. Larger discrepancies occur during the early heating stage and near the Curie temperature. Prediction times are reduced by three to four orders of magnitude with respect to a single finite element analysis. The results indicate that the proposed surrogate enables fast temperature estimation for optimization, digital twins, and closed-loop control of induction heating systems.
mech.title_ko: TEAM 문제 36의 비선형 자기-열 분석을 위한 딥러닝 대체 모델
mech.summary_ko:
- 유도 가열 시뮬레이션은 비선형 재료 특성 때문에 계산 비용이 높습니다.
- 이 연구는 컨볼루션 신경망 기반의 딥러닝 대체 모델을 제안합니다.
- 4만 개 이상의 유한요소(FE) 솔루션 데이터베이스를 구축하여 학습시켰습니다.
- 모델은 온도 분포를 6~10%의 평균 절대 백분율 오차로 예측합니다.
- 유도 가열 시스템의 최적화 및 제어에 활용 가능한 빠른 예측을 제공합니다.

papers.title: Deep Learning Surrogate Models for Nonlinear Magneto-Thermal Analysis of TEAM Problem 36
papers.summary: Induction heating is widely used in industrial processes such as forging, hardening, and additive manufacturing, but its accurate numerical simulation requires coupled electromagnetic and thermal finite element analyses with nonlinear, temperature-dependent material properties. This work proposes a deep learning surrogate model based on a convolu-tional neural network for TEAM Workshop Problem 36, a reference benchmark for nonlinear magneto-thermal induction heating. A database of more than 40,000 finite element solutions was generated by varying the supply current from 2 to 6 kA and the frequency from 2 to 6 kHz, while accounting for transient nonlinear effects, including the Curie transition. The network, composed of 24 layers with transposed convolutions, batch normalization, and dropout, maps current, frequency, and time to radial temperature distributions in the steel billet. For most operating conditions, the model achieves mean absolute percentage errors of about 6–7% for radial line in the middle of the billet and about 10% for radial line close to the billet end. Larger discrepancies occur during the early heating stage and near the Curie temperature. Prediction times are reduced by three to four orders of magnitude with respect to a single finite element analysis. The results indicate that the proposed surrogate enables fast temperature estimation for optimization, digital twins, and closed-loop control of induction heating systems.
